module_mood_contract: curious readiness — I've built enough to try something; let's see what the agent does with it

rules_evaluated.evidence: This lecture is an M4 module closer positioned after the send-off exercise, not a pre-exercise opener. The dosage rule governs openers before contrast-mood exercises; it does not apply here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture has no fenced prompts; forcing-function placement rule applies to exercises with copy-paste fences, not reflective lectures.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
Mood contract named and lands; resolving early steals next module's beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 mood_score: 8. Close mood_score: 8. Lecture fires after the send-off — M4 curious readiness mood contract honored. Company-layer question held deliberately open; M5's contrast-mood and M4→M5 unease gap preserved. No premature resolution.
The unease is 'nobody has cracked the shape yet' — not-knowing, not having-done-wrong. Company-layer open question is forward-facing curiosity, not a warning about mistakes.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture opens on the memory/context/rules naming discipline — positive precision ('Pick whichever word you'll defend. The label matters less than the two questions it hides'). No failure-mode framing in the lead.
